title: AI Knowledge Production

fields.vision: AI Knowledge Production is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument.
fields.mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.
fields.built_components: Visible public components: 57 kept Evidence Sections, native navigation, Review Notes, source-use plan, APA lineage, and source lineage claim limit. The public package remains text-focused and does not claim unverified runtime state.
fields.source_utilization: Method source. Clarifies the analytical method or reasoning discipline behind the page. Domain source. Supplies the substantive field knowledge against which claims must be checked. Evidence source. Supplies examples, cases, documents, or work products that make the page more than an assertion. Counter-source. Supplies rival interpretations and limits so the page does not become advocacy. A page-family source-use record exists. Final citation strength still depends on page-specific passage review.
fields.plan: Plan: separate prototype capability from validated finding, bind claims to test materials, expose false-positive and false-negative risks, and add method-specific source anchors. The project source notes below should be converted into authored subsections only after the page-specific claim limit and source-use decisions are settled.
fields.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the original project governance records. The page separates public presentation from project completion; completion depends on the status and remaining-gap fields.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: many AI pages need stronger page-specific experimental status, clearer validation boundaries, and less reliance on representative method bibliography. The kept Evidence Sections below preserve project substance for close reading; final public prose should be authored from those signals rather than copied mechanically.
fields.detailed_source_vision: AI Knowledge Production is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument. Its public purpose is to make AI system failure, retrieval integrity, analyst oversight, and model-governance problems legible as inspectable professional instruments. Source lead: Primary academic and government monographs. Includes George (2008, 2020), Lowenthal (2020), Heuer (1999), Lemieux (2026), Bullock (2024), Tradecraft Primer (2009), NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act, Stanford HAI Index, and 38 additional titles.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.

source_derived_signals.text: Open-source intelligence methodology from source identification through credibility assessment. Includes Zegart OSINT revolution thesis, Olcott information iceberg inversion, and Pashentsev adversarial narrative taxonomy.

Requirements, collection, processing, analysis, dissemination, feedback. The canonical six-stage model with variants from George, Lowenthal, and Fingar. Includes Lemieux augmentation points at each stage.

Nine core SATs from the Tradecraft Primer plus Beebe-Pherson extended set. Each technique: procedure, worked example, bias addressed, AI augmentation potential, and validation status from NRC/de Werd.
Raw source materials ingested from five source channels. Each channel has distinct extraction methods and quality gates. Total: 21,774 passages from 109 source files.

AI system evaluation along the loyalty chain: specification, verification, deployment, monitoring, accountability. Integrates Bullock AI loyalty, Lemieux governance-deployment gap, and CISA security controls.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It shows the utility of AI Knowledge Production by tying the project purpose to the mechanism that makes it inspectable, reusable, or professionally valuable.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
